title: embelia laeta subsp. papilligera
intro: Embelia laeta subsp. papilligera, also known as Indian black pepper vine, is a plant species that belongs to the family Myrsinaceae and is native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Embelia laeta subsp. papilligera include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and fruits are often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
